overview.service: Cloud computing, e-commerce, digital health services, and AI platform development
overview.consumer_base: Global enterprises, health systems, researchers, and direct-to-consumer health patients
overview.partnership_rationale: AWS HealthLake, Amazon Comprehend Medical, and Amazon Clinic position Amazon as a major health data infrastructure and consumer health player, creating partnership opportunities with UNC's informatics and public health research programs.
overview.primary_contact: AWS Research Credits Program / AWS Healthcare and Life Sciences team
overview.contact_reasoning: Amazon provides academic research credits through AWS Research Credits and has a dedicated Healthcare and Life Sciences division that partners with academic medical centers on data infrastructure projects.

unc_connection.donation_history: Amazon has provided AWS research credits to academic institutions. Verify specific UNC history via UNC Office of Development.

talking_points.detail: AWS HealthLake is a FHIR-compliant data lake service that enables health systems to store, query, and run ML models on large-scale patient data — directly applicable to UNC Health's research data infrastructure needs.

Amazon Comprehend Medical is a clinical NLP service that extracts structured data from unstructured clinical notes, lab reports, and medical records — a tool highly relevant to UNC's clinical informatics and NLP research pipelines.

Amazon Clinic provides direct-to-consumer telehealth and prescription services, generating longitudinal consumer health data and creating research questions around digital health access and equity that align with UNC's public health focus.
AWS offers academic research credits that provide UNC researchers with access to scalable cloud compute for health AI model training, genomics pipelines, and large-scale population health analysis.

talking_points.topic: Alexa Health Skills and Ambient Computing
talking_points.detail: Amazon's investment in voice-based health monitoring through Alexa Health Skills creates research opportunities around ambient home health sensing and chronic disease management that complement UNC's patient-centered informatics work.
